title: Why People in the UK Are Worried About Shopping
summary: A survey shows that people in the UK are feeling less confident about spending money because they are worried about a war between the US, Israel, and Iran. This war is causing problems like higher energy prices, which make things like petrol and household bills more expensive. As a result, shoppers are buying less, especially big items like furniture, and are saving more money instead. Experts say this fear might lead to slower economic growth in the UK. The survey found that confidence dropped a little in March, and people think the economy will not do well in the next year. This is a sign that spending could decrease more in the future.

Who said 'a ripple of fear is spreading'?

background_read: This article is about how people in the UK are changing their shopping habits because they are scared about a war far away. Wars can make things like petrol and electricity cost more, which means families have less money to spend on other things. When people save more and buy less, it can slow down the economy, which is how a country makes money. Surveys help experts understand what people are thinking so they can predict what might happen next.
Article_Structure: WHO: UK shoppers and experts like Neil Bellamy from GfK
WHAT: A decline in consumer confidence due to worries about a war affecting energy prices and spending
WHY: Because the war between the US, Israel, and Iran is causing higher costs and economic uncertainty

perspectives.description: People are saving more and spending less because they fear the war will hurt the economy.
